title: Eryngium larranagae
taxonomy: Eryngium larranagae is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Eryngium, which comprises a group of plants commonly known as eryngiums or sea hulles. The family Apiaceae, also known as the carrot or parsley family, is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many well-known species. Eryngium larranagae is native to South America, specifically in the Andean region. This species is often classified as a perennial herb, with a growth habit that varies depending on the environment and growing conditions.

intro: Eryngium larranagae, also known as Chilean sea holly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eryngium larranagae include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, often combined with other herbs to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
